author: ROME|King Bryan-Titan Analytics [STAR]
content:
I'd like to give a big shout-out to both the econ and game design teams on the new FIC2 Oni recipes. I've very impressed how similar the rewards are, despite being a completely different design and game loop.  And this sentence doesn't do it justice.  I've got a side-by-side comparison of all the fuel, ammo, raw materials, and SDUs  needed for both FIC1 and FIC2, and the work needed to balance all of these systems against each other, mining, crafting, freighting, scanning, is kind of mind boggling. And yet despite all of these complicated game loops... they feel balanced.

It feels like I'm rewarding for playing different, if I want to. I don't necessarily feel compelled to do FIC2 over FIC1.  However, if I pursue FIC2s more complicated structure, there is a modest gain over FIC1, but at the expense of way more complicated operations and burns.  And that feels GOOD.  And when I say modest gain its somewhere in the realm of 5%-10%. It also shifted the onus from crew to mining operations, so those with heavier mining fleets should do better with this recipe (At least for Oni).  Or those that want to focus on ships instead of crew.  And that is fantastic.

And to top all of this off... The team has to repeat this for 2 other factions.  Bravo.  And thank you.   

@Chri.z @Deleted User

author: Narr_nEoNONI
content:
Thank you always for your dedication and hard work

U.S. Crypto Market Regulations Are Easing, and Trends Are Changing.

However, I just confirmed a notice on Bitget that ATLAS will be delisted. (February 10th)

A few months ago, the team said that regarding listing on the exchange, we should move everything through DAO and community, not through the team (ATMTA).

Is the team still sensitive to regulatory issues? Should we just watch it get delisted? With a good market atmosphere, users can fully wait and cheer for the team's progress.

But we need to see the reality right now. All we can do is just wait for the slow progress of development? 

The project needs to be supported by constant support from users. To do so, I think we need to provide at least the right to remain in the project for users.

author: [AEP] Lerinor [STAR]
content:
Good morning, everyone! I have a question to raise, and I feel like @Chri.z is who I should tag for this, but let me know if I shouldn't.

I've been trying to come up with ways to make freighting (as a career) viable. But it always stumbles upon marketplace fees. I know they are an important part of the economy, but I realized that one of the main reasons it doesn't work (freighting) is because fees add up in a pretty bad "trickle up" way.

Player A (small) crafts 500 FICs/day. And can't freight it to redeeming bases. Player B (big) has 1 idle-ish Sledbarge, and offers to freight it for a small cost. Player A has 10k Atlas locked, pays 5.4% to sell to Player B, which has 560k Atlas locked, and then redeems FICs, paying 4.5%,

Roughly, 10% was lost during the whole operation. Not huge, but considering the margin for profit is thin (opportunity costs for Player A, for example, which could be just selling raw stuff), Sol fees, etc. etc., it kinda cripples it.

Which leads to potential OTC deals for FICs (yuck...).

Would it be possible to implement a fee exemption for FICs, when traded between players? The reason I see it working is because it has a fixed/pegged value, and would eventually have to be "sold" to ATMTA, and the originally intended fee would be collected anyway, thus not hurting the DAO/ATMTA revenue from it.

Just a thought, felt like asking/sharing.

Btw, I absolutely LOVED the new FICs update.

author: ROME | Ghaz OutroONI
content: Ship <> ship, and ship <> starbase. Claim stakes won't be attackable but if you take out the starbase then they're rendered useless. Some other scope we're tinkering with as far as claims/plot residual defenses but nothing locked in on how that plays out. Defense buildings won't be necessary for claims for C4 launch due to no direct attacks. But starbases are planned to have buildable defense structures.

reply.preview: Ship <> ship, and ship <> starbase. Claim stakes won't be attackable but if you take out the starbase then they're rendered useless. Some other scope we're tinkering with as far as claims/plot residual defenses but nothing locked in on how that plays out. Defense buildings won't be necessary for claims for C4 launch due to no direct attacks. But starbases are planned to have buildable defense structures.

author: bunthius [STAR]
content: I have a claim stake with building on it. The starbase gets destroyed and thus the building on it and my claim stake becomes "useless". Does this mean claim stake is destroyed and I loose it? OR do I get it back and have to place it on another starbase under my faction control and build it up again? Mainly can you please shed light on what happens to the CS once the starbase is destroyed.

reply.preview: I have a claim stake with building on it. The starbase gets destroyed and thus the building on it and my claim stake becomes "useless". Does this mean claim stake is destroyed and I loose it? OR do I get it back and have to place it on another starbase under my faction control and build it up again? Mainly can you please shed light on what happens to the CS once the starbase is destroyed.

author: ROME | Ghaz OutroONI
content: We haven't tested the combat reprecussions and balanced them yet but the plan is that you would get the claim stake back and a portion of the resources used to craft those buildings. Then you'd have to rebuild them in another location yes. In this hypothetical the incentive to defend starbases or expand the border regions are quite high. But with all things until we test it out we are still tinkering.

author: [Λ] Sawyn [BR] [STAR]
content:
Hi, I have a question about Z6. @SW4GN3R 

I remember that when the faction system started, each faction had two reference attributes, where there was "Influence over each other" like rock, paper, scissors.

This was lost over time, but some time ago it was asked how this would actually be implemented, and I believe it was Bunthius who said that it would have to do with the ease of obtaining some resources. For example: resources focused on damage for MUD, Shield for USTUR and Stealth for ONI.

My question is: Does Z6 fit into this part? Since it is the only base with R2 and H2 in copper, or is it an error due to the map rebalancing some time ago?

Don't get me wrong, I admit that I liked it because it was a MUD, but in the same way that we complained about not having the basic resources on the right side of the map, I think it is hypocritical not to point out that this is also a form of "imbalance" if it is not intentional as in the situation mentioned above.

Don't get me wrong, I admit that I liked it because it was a MUD, but in the same way that we complained about not having the basic resources on the right side of the map, I think it is hypocritical not to point out that this is also a form of "imbalance" if it is not intentional as in the situation mentioned above.

author: [AEP] Lerinor [STAR]
content:
@Chri.z expanding on my previous question:
Would it be possible to implement something like a "contracts dashboard"?

Player A requests 1m Frameworks to be delivered from base X to base Y.
Player B signs the contract, and has (...) days to fulfill it.
Player A provides 1m Frameworks to an escrow account, player B picks it up at X, drops at Y.

Contract is filled, Player B receives payment from Player A, items are delivered. No fees incurred in the process, making it viable, while also fomenting a whole new branch of activities in the ecosystem.

author: Gary Sanchez (AD Veh) [STAR]
content: Actual scaling yes for sage  , i need to check with team but probably yes ,  but those will not be translated in UE5 for scale , because in UE5 prod , more we harmonizing and integrating Spec interior in Ships , ergonomy , swappable modules or others , more ships have a tendency to be extended in length , and also between class to mark more difference , so for example Om was extended to 50 meter length in UE5 , to disrupt more from S class almost around 30-35 m now , and to be more like other M such F4 and Opod around 50-55 meter , and cargo space , same probably for large class in futur, capital ships for example with C9 was orginally 400m in concept and more than 460-480m in prod then , we recently also extend a bit rear of R6 for more cargo space integration.
